title: Lisa The Beauty Queen

jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Homer presumably crashes in opening sequence
jokes.punchline: His signature exclamation of pain/frustration
jokes.explanation: Homer's signature catchphrase - perfectly character-integrated and economical but routine by this point in the series

jokes.text: Principal Skinner, 'The Happiest Place on Earth' is a registered Disneyland copyright.
jokes.setup: School carnival apparently uses Disney's slogan
jokes.punchline: Corporate lawyer immediately threatens lawsuit over minor infringement
jokes.explanation: Sharp corporate satire about Disney's litigious nature, well-structured but not character-specific

jokes.text: Well, so did you. You got an ex-Green Beret mad.
jokes.setup: Lawyer says Skinner made a big mistake
jokes.punchline: Skinner reveals his military background as implied threat
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - reveals Skinner's military past while creating tension reversal. Great economy and structure.

jokes.text: [Groaning, Gagging] [Gasps] [Groans] Copyright expired. And... begin!
jokes.setup: Tension between ex-military Skinner and lawyer
jokes.punchline: Skinner apparently physically intimidates lawyer until copyright expires
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy payoff to the Green Beret setup, though the resolution is somewhat unclear

jokes.text: This sucks. Aah! Ha-ha! Pick the red, get ahead. Pick the black, set you back.
jokes.setup: Typical carnival game pitch
jokes.punchline: Bart's blunt assessment followed by immediate consequences
jokes.explanation: Bart's characteristic irreverence meets immediate comeuppance, but fairly standard

jokes.text: Get your haggis right here! Chopped heart and lungs... boiled in a wee sheep's stomach! Tastes as good as it sounds! Good for what ails ya!
jokes.setup: Carnival food vendor pitch
jokes.punchline: Disgusting description followed by ironic 'tastes as good as it sounds'
jokes.explanation: Willie's Scottish authenticity makes the gross-out food funny, self-aware irony works well

jokes.text: I'd say... 53 years old and 420 pounds. Ha-ha, you lose! 36 and 239.
jokes.setup: Age/weight guessing game
jokes.punchline: Homer is much younger but still extremely overweight
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer weight joke with good misdirection - he's younger than expected but still massively overweight

jokes.text: Challenge accepted!
jokes.setup: Bart dares Otto to make ride go faster
jokes.punchline: Otto immediately accepts dangerous dare with enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Perfect Otto characterization - reckless and enthusiastic

jokes.text: I'll be in Mexico till this thing blows over.
jokes.setup: Otto makes ride go dangerously fast
jokes.punchline: Immediate plan to flee the country
jokes.explanation: Perfect Otto line - reckless followed by cowardly escape plan. Excellent economy and character voice.

jokes.text: Hand over all your money. Was it scary? Uh-huh. Ooh, baby.
jokes.setup: Spookhouse experience
jokes.punchline: It's actually just robbery, kid confirms it was scary
jokes.explanation: Dark twist on carnival attraction - the 'spook' is actual crime. Well-structured with good economy.

jokes.text: So, girlie, you like roller-skating? No. Yeah, everybody loves roller-skating. Okay, you can turn around now. [Gasps] [Spectators Laughing]
jokes.setup: Lisa gets caricature drawn
jokes.punchline: Unflattering cartoon makes her gasp in horror
jokes.explanation: Sets up Lisa's insecurity about looks - important plot setup but not a strong standalone joke

jokes.text: Oh, my God! I'm ugly!
jokes.setup: Lisa sees her caricature
jokes.punchline: Devastating self-realization
jokes.explanation: Lisa's earnest reaction drives the episode's emotional core, economical but predictable

jokes.text: You see the circular pattern on those fields? That's from central-pivot irrigation. Wow. Now let's see what's happening at the Super Bowl. [Crowd Cheering] Homer! Homer! Homer! Homer! Oops. Sorry. Homer! Homer! Homer! Homer! Homer! Homer!
jokes.setup: Homer fantasizes about blimp ride with educational narration
jokes.punchline: Fantasy shifts to crowd chanting his name at Super Bowl, then apology, then continued chanting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer fantasy - starts educational, becomes megalomaniacal. The 'oops sorry' and continued chanting is brilliant character work.

jokes.text: Ned Flanders! [Gasps] Ah! Go, Ned! Oh, it's no fair. We'll never have a buffer. We have one at home. You never use it. Well, I want that one.
jokes.setup: Ned wins second prize shoe buffer
jokes.punchline: Homer's jealousy despite having unused buffer at home
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer jealousy of Ned, with Marge's practical deflation. Good character interaction.

jokes.text: And the winner of the blimp ride is... Homer Simpson! Oh, my God!
jokes.setup: Blimp ride raffle winner announced
jokes.punchline: Homer's dream comes true with characteristic overreaction
jokes.explanation: Sets up Homer's sacrifice later, but routine excitement reaction

jokes.text: Oh, Lisa, this isn't real. It's just how you might look If you were a cartoon character. I'm an ugmo.
jokes.setup: Homer tries to comfort Lisa about caricature
jokes.punchline: Meta joke about them being cartoon characters, Lisa rejects comfort
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-humor - Homer doesn't realize they ARE cartoon characters. Highly original and clever writing.

jokes.text: Now, that's not true. You're cute as a bug's ear. Fathers have to say that stuff. Dad, am I cute as a bug's ear? No! You're homely as a mule's butt! There. See?
jokes.setup: Homer gives fatherly reassurance, Lisa questions its validity
jokes.punchline: Bart demonstrates with brutal honesty to Homer
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup and payoff structure. Bart's brutal honesty proves Lisa's point about parental bias. Excellent economy.

jokes.text: Moe, have you ever felt unattractive? Mmm, no. How about you, Barney? Not for a second. [Belches] Yeah. I need help.
jokes.setup: Homer asks obviously unattractive men about feeling unattractive
jokes.punchline: Both confidently say no, Homer realizes he needs help
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - the least attractive characters have total confidence. Homer's realization is the cherry on top.

jokes.text: [Inhales, Exhales] Ahh. That sweet Carolina smoke. But they can't prohibit us from holding a beauty pageant... for little girls age seven to nine.
jokes.setup: Cigarette executive on TV can't advertise
jokes.punchline: Exploiting children's beauty pageant as advertising loophole
jokes.explanation: Sharp corporate satire about exploiting advertising loopholes through children. Dark but well-crafted.

jokes.text: I heartily endorse this event or product.
jokes.setup: Krusty appears in cigarette commercial
jokes.punchline: Generic endorsement showing he'll shill for anything
jokes.explanation: Perfect Krusty - mercenary and generic. The vague 'event or product' is beautifully economical.

jokes.text: What a feeling! I'm as happy as a smoker... taking that first puff in the morning.
jokes.setup: Krusty expresses happiness
jokes.punchline: Compares it to cigarette addiction
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy about addiction presented as positive feeling. Good structure and character voice.

jokes.text: Hey, Barney, will you give me 250 bucks for this blimp ticket? Sure! [Gasps] Where'd you get all the money? From some scientist. Since they stopped testing on animals... a guy like me can really clean up.
jokes.setup: Homer needs money, Barney has lots
jokes.punchline: Barney is human test subject since animal testing stopped
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - animal rights progress leads to human exploitation. Perfect for Barney's character and very original.

jokes.text: Lisa, I know a song that will cheer you up. [Singing] So you think I'm ugly? No! No, I meant you were one Of the good-looking ducks... that makes fun of the ugly one.
jokes.setup: Homer tries to cheer Lisa with Ugly Duckling song
jokes.punchline: Makes it worse by casting her as the mean duck
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer parenting fail - makes consolation worse through misunderstanding. Great character integration and cringe factor.

jokes.text: Lisa, if you could have one wish, what would it be? To shut myself off from the world and never be seen by human eyes again. Was your second wish to be entered in the Little Miss Springfield Pageant?
jokes.setup: Lisa expresses desire to hide from world
jokes.punchline: Homer asks if pageant was second wish - complete opposite
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic structure - pageant is literally opposite of what Lisa wants. Homer's obliviousness is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: I couldn't find a big enough photo, so I sent in... that funny drawing of you on roller skates.
jokes.setup: Homer explains pageant application
jokes.punchline: Used the unflattering caricature that started Lisa's crisis
jokes.explanation: Good callback to opening crisis, Homer's obliviousness makes bad situation worse

jokes.text: Dad, have you seen the girls they have in those contests? Hubba, hubba! [Panting] [Howling]
jokes.setup: Lisa points out pageant competition
jokes.punchline: Homer's over-the-top lustful reaction
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer horndog behavior, but the wolf-howling pushes it into cartoonish territory

jokes.text: Well, if I could gouge out somebody else's eyes... and shove them into my sockets, I would. But to me, she's beautiful.
jokes.setup: Marge says Homer sees Lisa through father's eyes
jokes.punchline: Homer's grotesque solution followed by sweet sentiment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer line - starts horrifyingly violent then becomes genuinely sweet. The contrast is perfect and highly quotable.

jokes.text: But do you know how your father got the money to enter you in this pageant? Nope. He sold his ride on the Duff blimp. But that ride meant everything to him!
jokes.setup: Marge reveals Homer's sacrifice
jokes.punchline: Lisa realizes the depth of his fantasy and sacrifice
jokes.explanation: Good emotional callback to Homer's fantasy, motivates Lisa's decision to participate

jokes.text: Did you see Tina Epstein? Whoa! If you're gonna binge, you'd better purge. Uh-oh.
jokes.setup: Commenting on pageant contestant
jokes.punchline: Reference to eating disorders in children's beauty pageants
jokes.explanation: Dark but economical satire of pageant culture and eating disorders among children

jokes.text: Amber Dempsey. In the same week... she was Pork Princess and Little Miss Kosher.
jokes.setup: Introducing star contestant Amber
jokes.punchline: Won contradictory titles in same week (pork vs kosher)
jokes.explanation: Clever contradiction highlighting absurdity of pageant circuit - pork and kosher are mutually exclusive

jokes.text: Eyelash implants. I thought those were illegal. Not in Paraguay.
jokes.setup: Amber reveals extreme beauty enhancement
jokes.punchline: Legal loophole through foreign country
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of pageant extremes, Paraguay reference adds specificity

jokes.text: She's the Jack Nicklaus Of the pageant circuit. Oh, I don't know that she's as attractive as Jack Nicklaus. I meant because they both win all the time, Mom. Oh. Well, yes. That's what I meant too.
jokes.setup: Lisa compares Amber to golf champion Jack Nicklaus
jokes.punchline: Marge misunderstands, thinks Lisa means attractiveness, awkward recovery
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection comedy with Marge's confusion and awkward backpedaling

jokes.text: And later, I'll teach you the tricks of the trade-- taping your swimsuit to your butt... petroleum jelly on your teeth for that frictionless smile... and the ancient art of padding.
jokes.setup: Marge offers to teach pageant tricks
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd and uncomfortable beauty hacks
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of pageant artifice, 'frictionless smile' is particularly clever phrasing

jokes.text: Boy, when Lisa comes in, I want you to make a big fuss over how she looks. She'll see through me like Grandma's underpants.
jokes.setup: Homer instructs Bart to compliment Lisa
jokes.punchline: Bart's crude but accurate analogy about transparency
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart analogy - crude but apt. The specificity of 'Grandma's underpants' makes it memorable.

jokes.text: When it comes to compliments... women are ravenous, bloodsucking monsters... always wanting more, more, more! And if you give it to 'em, you'll get plenty back in return. Like what? I'll tell you when you're older.
jokes.setup: Homer explains women's need for compliments
jokes.punchline: Horrific description followed by sexual innuendo cutoff
jokes.explanation: Homer's crude but typical relationship advice, 'tell you when you're older' is classic parent dodge

jokes.text: Is that Lisa? Ooh, I gotta call heaven. There's an angel missing. And who's your little school friend? Wait a minute. That's Mom!
jokes.setup: Bart delivers over-the-top compliment to Lisa
jokes.punchline: Mistakes made-up Marge for Lisa's friend, then realizes error
jokes.explanation: Good structure showing transformation effectiveness, Bart's mistake adds humor layer

jokes.text: I know two fellas who are gonna get a special dinner tonight. Hey, hey! Whoo-hoo! Dad, you know anything else about women? Nope, that's it.
jokes.setup: Marge promises special dinner, Homer celebrates
jokes.punchline: Bart asks for more wisdom, Homer admits total ignorance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer honesty - his one trick worked so well he admits that's all he knows. Good economy.

jokes.text: Hey, brush-head, you've been nursing that thing for an hour. You know, I was just wondering how someone who works at an ice cream store... keeps such a trim figure. I've misjudged you.
jokes.setup: Moe is rude to Lisa about slow ice cream eating
jokes.punchline: Lisa's sophisticated compliment completely disarms him
jokes.explanation: Great reversal - Lisa's charm offensive works on gruff Moe. Shows her learning Homer's lesson.

jokes.text: I said step, pause, turn, pause, pivot, step, step... not step, pause, turn, pause, pivot, step, pause! Ooh! Shudder!
jokes.setup: Pageant coach gives precise walking instructions
jokes.punchline: Nitpicky correction and disgusted reaction
jokes.explanation: Decent pageant culture satire showing absurd precision required, but not particularly strong

jokes.text: And the winner is... Lisa Simpson! [Gasps] Okay, wipe away a tear. Hug the loser. And now, for your triumphant walk down the runway. Whoa! Ooh! Aah! It's hopeless!
jokes.setup: Lisa wins practice round, gets runway instructions
jokes.punchline: Immediately falls/stumbles, coach despairs
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy showing Lisa's inexperience, functional but not exceptional

jokes.text: There's nothin' to it. Give me those heels. Heel, toe, heel, toe, heel, toe. Hmm. Do you really think I can win? Hey, I'm starting to think I can win.
jokes.setup: Marge demonstrates walking, Lisa asks about winning chances
jokes.punchline: Marge gets carried away and thinks she could win
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Marge getting caught up in pageant excitement

jokes.text: Lise, as your brother... this is the hardest thing I've every had to say. You're not ugly. Oh, Bart!
jokes.setup: Bart builds up something difficult to say
jokes.punchline: It's just saying she's not ugly (which is hard for him to be nice)
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart moment - being nice is genuinely difficult for him. Great character integration and emotional setup.

jokes.text: My name is Amber Dempsey, and when I grow up I want to be a sweetie pie.
jokes.setup: Pageant contestant introduction
jokes.punchline: Vapid, meaningless career aspiration
jokes.explanation: Standard pageant culture satire showing empty responses, economical but predictable

jokes.text: I am Pahasatira Nahasapeemapetilon... and tonight I will be playing 'Mac Arthur Park' on the tabla. [Laughing] No, I am serious. Yes! Judges, there's your queen. Who's watching the Kwik-E-Mart? [Both Gasp]
jokes.setup: Apu's niece announces unusual talent performance
jokes.punchline: Audience laughs thinking it's a joke, Apu's pride and panic about store
jokes.explanation: Good cultural humor and Apu's characteristic work anxiety. The serious delivery of absurd talent is effective.

jokes.text: Yes! Judges, there's your queen. - Who's watching the Kwik-E-Mart?
jokes.setup: Apu excited about his niece's performance
jokes.punchline: Suddenly realizes he left work unattended
jokes.explanation: Good Apu character moment with sudden practical realization

jokes.text: My name is Lisa Simpson, and I want to be Little Miss Springfield... so I can make our town a better place! Yeah! Clean up this stink-hole! [Panting, Grunts]
jokes.setup: Lisa gives earnest civic-minded introduction
jokes.punchline: Audience member's crude agreement and overenthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Lisa's idealism and crude audience response

jokes.text: Yeah! Clean up this stink-hole!
jokes.setup: Lisa wants to make Springfield a better place
jokes.punchline: Audience member's crude agreement
jokes.explanation: Undermines Lisa's eloquence with crude Springfield reality

jokes.text: Krusty! About time you got here. Yeah, yeah. What is this-- the, uh, Republican fund-raiser?
jokes.setup: Krusty arrives late to host pageant
jokes.punchline: Mistakes pageant for political event (both have similar demographics)
jokes.explanation: Sharp political/demographic observation delivered in Krusty's typical disengaged style

jokes.text: Oh, that brisket's not sittin' right. [Snoring] Oh, that just kept goin', huh?
jokes.setup: Krusty has stomach problems during performance
jokes.punchline: Falls asleep, wakes up confused about extended performance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Krusty unprofessionalism - physical discomfort and inattention during hosting duties

jokes.text: Oh, that just kept goin', huh?
jokes.setup: After long performance/song
jokes.punchline: Krusty's awkward acknowledgment of tedious length
jokes.explanation: Good awkward host moment, relatable reaction

jokes.text: Some people say that to love your country is old-fashioned... uncool, real melvin. Well, to them I say-- [Singing Patriotic Song] One, two, three, four! [Singing Rock And Roll Song]
jokes.setup: Lisa begins earnest patriotic speech
jokes.punchline: Transitions from sincere patriotism to rock performance
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lisa's ability to connect with audience by mixing idealism with entertainment

jokes.text: This is my favorite part Of the pageant, because-- What does that say? Oh. It gives me a chance to visit with the girls personality. Uh, personally.
jokes.setup: Krusty introduces interview segment
jokes.punchline: Can't read teleprompter, mixes up words awkwardly
jokes.explanation: Classic Krusty incompetence - illiterate and inappropriate word choices

jokes.text: Amber, do you think the Bill of Rights... is a good thing or a bad thing? Um-- Take your time, dear. Good thing.
jokes.setup: Krusty asks about fundamental American document
jokes.punchline: Amber struggles with incredibly basic question
jokes.explanation: Good satire of pageant intelligence standards - Bill of Rights should be obvious 'good thing'

jokes.text: And don't say it'll never happen... because we all remember that thing with what's-her-name. Click, click. You know.
jokes.setup: Explaining runner-up role in case winner can't fulfill duties
jokes.punchline: Vague reference to famous scandal (implied Miss America controversy)
jokes.explanation: Clever reference to real pageant scandals without naming specifics, 'click, click' suggests camera/photos

jokes.text: Hi. Can I drive? Well, I can't see the harm. [Barney Yelling] Oh, the humanity!
jokes.setup: Child beauty queen asks to drive blimp
jokes.punchline: Immediate disaster with Hindenburg reference
jokes.explanation: Great escalation and historical reference. Barney's presence from earlier blimp purchase pays off perfectly.

jokes.text: Oh, dear. It would be a shame If that pretty dress got wet. I'd say the greater danger is her scepter acting as a lightning rod. Unless it's made out of plastic. [Thunderclap] Nope. Metal.
jokes.setup: Commentators worry about rain on dress
jokes.punchline: Escalates to lightning rod concern, confirmed by immediate strike
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation structure - minor concern becomes major danger becomes immediate reality. Perfect timing.

jokes.text: Oh, she'll be fine. In fact... she's already won the Little Miss Intensive Care Pageant.
jokes.setup: Doctor reports on lightning strike victim's condition
jokes.punchline: Dark joke about hospital pageant
jokes.explanation: Dark but clever medical humor, 'Little Miss Intensive Care' is inspired wordplay

jokes.text: Here's your scepter. Oop! [Laughing] just kidding. Here you go. Oop! [Laughing] just kidding. [Yelling] I deserved that.
jokes.setup: Doctor playfully fake-drops scepter twice
jokes.punchline: Lisa's violent reaction, doctor's acknowledgment
jokes.explanation: Standard slapstick with good self-awareness in the 'I deserved that' admission

jokes.text: That doesn't look like her body. The torso used to be Dr. Ruth. Her head is on a pike in the Chamber of Horrors.
jokes.setup: Looking at wax figure display
jokes.punchline: Grotesque explanation of figure construction
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny behind-the-scenes look at wax museum construction, Dr. Ruth reference adds specificity

jokes.text: Love that 'chewing gum' walk. Very Wrigley.
jokes.setup: Lisa walking in her role as spokesperson
jokes.punchline: Comments on her walk and makes gum brand pun
jokes.explanation: Decent pun combining walk description with brand name

jokes.text: As Little Miss Springfield, it's my pleasure to welcome you to America... the land of opportunity. Actually, they're being deported.
jokes.setup: Lisa gives welcoming speech about America
jokes.punchline: People are actually being kicked out
jokes.explanation: Sharp ironic contrast between American ideals and harsh reality. Excellent structure and economy.

jokes.text: Hello, this is Bob 'What the hell am I doing in Springfield' Hope. Hey, how about that Mayor Quimby? He's some golfer! His golfball spends more time underwater than Greg Louganis.
jokes.setup: Bob Hope doing USO-style performance
jokes.punchline: Self-aware confusion about location and local political humor
jokes.explanation: Good Bob Hope impression with meta-humor and period-appropriate references (Louganis)

jokes.text: His golfball spends more time underwater than Greg Louganis.
jokes.setup: Bob Hope doing standard political roast humor
jokes.punchline: Comparing bad golf to Olympic diver
jokes.explanation: Standard Bob Hope-style joke structure, competent but not exceptional

jokes.text: Little Miss Springfield? First, Tony Randall cancels. Now this. Hey, what gives here? This is bogus! Hey! Where do you think you're goin'? We want Miss Springfield! Hey, set me down at that boat show.
jokes.setup: Bob Hope sees Lisa instead of expected beauty queen
jokes.punchline: Professional disappointment escalates to troop complaints and escape request
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of disappointment, Tony Randall reference adds authenticity to Hope's era

jokes.text: I'm gonna be riding on a pack of cigarettes? Mm-hmm. We think you're the perfect spokesmodel for Laramies. It's part of our new campaign. Mmm, classy. [Gasps]
jokes.setup: Lisa discovers she'll be on cigarette packaging
jokes.punchline: Her sarcastic 'classy' and horror at realization
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Lisa's moral crisis, her sarcastic response is perfectly in character

jokes.text: You see, Lisa, it's been an unlucky year for Laramie. A lot of the people who smoke our product have been... well, dying. And we need young smokers to take their place.
jokes.setup: Executive explains business situation
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of killing customers and targeting children
jokes.explanation: Dark corporate satire with casual admission of evil business model. Well-structured reveal.

jokes.text: And we're thinking of retiring Menthol Moose. [Coughs, Spits]
jokes.setup: Executive mentions retiring mascot
jokes.punchline: Mascot is visibly dying from smoking
jokes.explanation: Dark visual gag - even the cartoon mascot is suffering from the product. Highly original concept.

jokes.text: Bless you, boys. Homer, those are ice cream men. I know.
jokes.setup: Homer appears to mistake ice cream men for something else
jokes.punchline: He knows exactly what they are but salutes anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - he salutes ice cream men because he respects their service to his appetite. Great economy.

jokes.text: Stop this float! [Tires Screech] What's the holdup? Go! I'm tired of being a corporate shill. [Grunts] From now on, I will speak out... against the evils in society... from dog-napping to cigarettes.
jokes.setup: Lisa stops parade float to make announcement
jokes.punchline: Declares war on various social evils, mixing serious with trivial
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa moment - moral awakening with characteristic mixing of major and minor issues

jokes.text: Before I sing the national anthem... I'd like to say that college football... diverts funds badly needed for education and the arts. Is that true? Let's get 'em! [Grunting] Let's get out of here!
jokes.setup: Lisa makes controversial statement at football game
jokes.punchline: Crowd turns violent immediately, escape necessary
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing consequences of Lisa's activism. The crowd's instant violence is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: But this glorified, crossing-guard-of-a-police Chief won't get off his big fat can. Is it okay if I open these potato chips? [Groans] Gentlemen, our prayers have been answered.
jokes.setup: Executive complains about unhelpful police chief
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's trivial concern proves their point exactly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum incompetence - proves executive's point while being completely oblivious

jokes.text: Lisa Simpson is no longer Little Miss Springfield. She was stripped of her crown in a ceremony earlier today. Well, that's obviously the wrong footage. Uh, but it does seem the father of the deposed beauty queen, Homer Simpson... filled out the pageant application incorrectly. In the area under 'Do not write in this space,' he wrote, 'Okay.'
jokes.setup: News report about Lisa losing crown
jokes.punchline: Homer's literal-minded rule-breaking with 'Okay'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer moment - perfectly logical response to 'do not write' instruction. Highly original and character-specific.

jokes.text: Dad, do you remember why you entered me in that pageant? I don't know. Was I drunk? Possibly. But the point is, you wanted me to feel better about myself, and I do.
jokes.setup: Lisa asks about Homer's motivations
jokes.punchline: Homer's honest uncertainty about his sobriety, but Lisa finds meaning anyway
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's self-awareness about his drinking while maintaining emotional sincerity

jokes.text: And now, my exclusive interview with His Holiness, Pope John Paul I. That's it. I cannot work under these conditions. If anybody wants me, I'll be downstairs at McDougal's. Call the weekend guy. I don't care.
jokes.setup: Anchor announces prestigious interview
jokes.punchline: Immediately quits in frustration, abandons professional duties
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing complete professional breakdown. The Pope interview makes the abandonment more absurd.

jokes.text: That's it. I cannot work under these conditions. If anybody wants me, I'll be downstairs at McDougal's.
jokes.setup: News anchor dealing with technical problems
jokes.punchline: Dramatic exit to bar during live broadcast
jokes.explanation: Good professional meltdown humor, escalation to abandoning job

tmdb_overview: Homer enters Lisa into the Little Miss Springfield Pageant to help her feel better about her looks. But when she must choose between the title and her integrity, she realizes that confidence comes from within.
insight: Lisa's pageant arc delivers steady character comedy at 3.87 jokes per minute across seventy-five gags.
